findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five bullet points)
N/A — entire plan
AC: 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' and 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding:
The plan introduces file I/O and an external shell script call (suggestion-record.sh) as failure points, but the reliability story is underspecified in two ways:

(a) error_handling — The AC only guarantees 'no crash' on malformed sentinel input. It does not address what happens if suggestion-record.sh itself fails (non-zero exit, missing executable, permission error, disk full). There is no mention of retry, graceful degradation, or whether a failure in suggestion-record.sh should be surfaced to the caller or silently swallowed. A write failure in suggestion-record.sh with no handling is a MAJOR gap.

(b) failover / idempotency — The plan does not clarify whether calling suggestion-record.sh multiple times for the same Agent tool return (e.g., hook re-runs due to dispatcher retry) is safe. If the underlying record store is append-only, duplicate SUGGESTION entries could be created without idempotency guards. The plan is silent on this.

These cannot be resolved without inspecting suggestion-record.sh and the dispatcher retry behaviour.
findings.suggested_edit:
Add to the Acceptance Criteria:
1. 'If suggestion-record.sh exits non-zero, the hook logs a warning and exits 0 (hook must not propagate record-write failures to the caller).'
2. 'Calling the hook twice for the same tool-return is idempotent (or the design explicitly documents and accepts duplicate recording).' 
Document the chosen error-handling contract in the hook's inline comments.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (PostToolUse hook) that crosses component boundaries: it couples the hook dispatcher, suggestion-record.sh, and the Agent tool return pipeline. Three sub-checks raise concerns: (a) Coupling risk — the dependency on suggestion-record.sh is named but not justified or mitigated (no interface or event boundary described; the '(or post-all.sh)' uncertainty suggests the exact integration point is unresolved at plan time). (b) Changeability — the sentinel format 'SUGGESTION:' is hardcoded with no mention of making it configurable, even though sentinel formats are precisely the kind of rule that evolves. (c) Documentation — the plan introduces a novel architectural decision (a new hook stage + CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher pattern extension) but mentions no ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update to capture it. All three sub-checks flag real gaps.
findings.suggested_edit: (a) Resolve the 'post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh' ambiguity before implementation and document the chosen integration point with a rationale. (b) Make the sentinel prefix (e.g. 'SUGGESTION:') a named constant or environment variable so it can be changed without editing the hook. (c) Add an acceptance criterion: 'Update CLAUDE.md / relevant ADR to document the new PostToolUse hook stage and its contract with suggestion-record.sh.'

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria sections: 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh', 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)', 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher', 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: Multiple sub-checks fail: (a) The only concrete proving command given is 'bash tests/run-all.sh'. No proving command is provided for: (i) verifying the hook is actually registered in the dispatcher (AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'), (ii) confirming suggestion-record.sh is actually called with the correct arguments at runtime (not just that tests pass), or (iii) demonstrating the malformed-sentinel warning appears without a crash. (b) ACs 2–4 contain no described implementation step, only a label — the union of steps does not reach these end-states. (c) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' is too vague: no edge cases are enumerated (empty return, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, malformed format variants), so there is no way to verify completeness. (d) There is no end-to-end check showing the hook fires in a real Agent tool return flow, and no documented rationale for its absence. The single 'bash tests/run-all.sh' command is a universal test sweep that does not by itself prove any specific criterion.
findings.suggested_edit: Add per-AC proving commands: (1) for hook registration, add a grep/inspection command such as 'grep -r PostToolUse plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/'; (2) for suggestion-record.sh invocation, add a test fixture that captures the call and asserts arguments; (3) enumerate the malformed-sentinel edge cases explicitly (e.g., line missing text after colon, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, no SUGGESTION: line) and add an AC asserting each is handled; (4) add an integration-level smoke test or document why a full end-to-end test is deferred.

findings.location: Summary vs. Acceptance Criteria — 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' / 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding:
Two potential issues:

(a) Partial orphan: The Summary names two candidate files — 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — but no acceptance criterion pins which file must be modified. 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' is too vague to verify which file was actually changed, leaving the file-target ambiguous between description and criteria.

(b) Terminology drift: The Summary references the 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' as part of the integration context, but this term appears nowhere in the acceptance criteria. Whether this pattern must be followed is described as context but never verified. It is unclear if this is an implied constraint or informational background.

(c) No duplicate criteria detected.

(d) The 'malformed sentinel' warning criterion in AC ('Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)') is coherently covered by the description ('Warn on malformed sentinel').

No outright missing-deliverable or measuring-nothing criterion found, but the 'or' in the file target and the untested CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN constraint reduce verifiability.
findings.suggested_edit: Replace 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' with 'Hook is registered in plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh [or whichever file is chosen] and invoked by the consolidated dispatcher'. Also either add a criterion verifying adherence to the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern or remove the reference from the Summary if it is purely informational.

findings.location: All acceptance criteria
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'
findings.finding:
The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (PostToolUse hook) that crosses at least two component boundaries: (1) the dispatcher (post-bash.sh or post-all.sh), (2) the suggestion-record.sh utility, and (3) the Agent tool return mechanism. Three maintainability sub-checks are at risk:

(a) Coupling risk: The plan introduces a new dependency from the dispatcher onto suggestion-record.sh and onto the Agent tool's return format (the SUGGESTION: sentinel contract). Neither the interface between the dispatcher and suggestion-record.sh nor the sentinel format contract is described or justified — it is silently assumed both exist and are stable.

(b) Changeability: The sentinel format ('SUGGESTION:') is treated as a hardcoded string with no mention of configurability. If the sentinel prefix ever changes, the extraction logic must be found and updated in the hook source. The plan gives no indication this is configurable or even documented.

(c) Documentation: The plan introduces a new architectural pattern (PostToolUse hook extracting sentinels via a dispatcher) but references no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc change. The story reference '.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4' is not a design artifact accessible in this review.
findings.suggested_edit: Add to the plan: (1) A brief description of the interface/contract between the dispatcher and suggestion-record.sh (arguments, exit codes, expected behavior). (2) Either make the SUGGESTION: sentinel prefix configurable (e.g., via an env var or config file) or explicitly document that it is intentionally fixed and reference where that decision is recorded. (3) Call out that a CLAUDE.md or ADR entry will be added describing the PostToolUse hook pattern and its sentinel contract.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section — all five ACs
findings.finding:
Several acceptance criteria lack concrete proving commands or steps, and the union of described steps does not fully reach the stated end-state:

(a) AC: 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh' — No proving command is given. The single top-level check ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is a universal test runner, which per the anti-FP rule does not by itself prove this specific extraction behavior. No step describes how one would isolate and observe that extraction occurs correctly.

(b) AC: 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' — No step or test scenario is described that feeds a malformed sentinel to the hook and verifies the warning output and non-zero-but-non-crash exit behavior.

(c) AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' — No step verifies registration (e.g., grepping the dispatcher file for the hook, or a test that the dispatcher invokes the hook).

(d) AC: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — The plan names this AC but lists no edge cases (e.g., multiple SUGGESTION: lines, empty return, sentinel mid-line, missing SUGGESTION: entirely). Without enumerating them, it is impossible to judge whether the tests actually reach this criterion.

(e) The plan hedges implicitly: 'should work' is not written, but the only verification anchor ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is not mapped to each AC — it is a single command covering all, which leaves ACs (b), (c), (d) unmeasured by any described step.
findings.suggested_edit:
For each AC, add a concrete proving command or scenario:
- AC 'extracts first SUGGESTION:': Add a dedicated test script or bats/bash test case name, e.g., 'tests/hooks/test-post-tool-use-suggestion.sh — run with a fixture that contains a SUGGESTION: line and assert suggestion-record.sh is called with the correct argument'.
- AC 'warns on malformed': Add a test scenario: 'feed input like SUGGESTION: (no value) or SUGGESTION:no-space and assert stderr contains a warning and exit code is 0'.
- AC 'registered in dispatcher': Add a step: 'grep post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh) for the hook name as part of CI or a lint check'.
- AC 'edge cases': Enumerate at least: (1) no SUGGESTION: line present, (2) multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first taken), (3) SUGGESTION: at end of output, (4) malformed sentinel.

findings.location: Summary + Acceptance Criteria (5 items)
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: The plan wavers on the exact target file ('post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh') without resolving which dispatcher is correct for intercepting Agent tool returns. Agent tool returns are not Bash tool returns, so routing to post-bash.sh could be the wrong hook entirely. This ambiguity means the agent may build the hook in the wrong dispatcher, which would satisfy the letter of the AC ('registered in consolidated dispatcher') while missing the actual call-site for Agent tool completions. Without access to the existing dispatcher architecture it cannot be confirmed which file is correct, but the 'or' phrasing signals unresolved design uncertainty that should be pinned before execution.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve the dispatcher target before execution: inspect the consolidated dispatcher architecture to confirm which hook file fires on Agent (sub-agent) tool returns specifically (likely a post-tool-use or post-all dispatcher, not post-bash). Update the Summary to name exactly one file and remove the 'or' hedge.

findings.location: Summary + Acceptance Criteria (5 ACs)
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: The plan is broadly faithful to its title ('Implement PostToolUse hook to extract SUGGESTION: sentinel from sub-agent returns') but carries two small ambiguities that could cause the agent to build the wrong thing: (1) The target file is stated as 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — both options are left open, and the correct one depends on which dispatcher fires for Agent tool returns vs. Bash tool returns. Without resolving this, the hook may be registered in the wrong dispatcher and never trigger. (2) The AC says 'first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return' but does not specify what 'Agent tool' means precisely (e.g., the tool_name filter to match). Neither issue is a full intent-contradiction, but both are underspecified enough to produce a different artifact from what is intended.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve the dispatcher file choice before implementation begins: confirm whether Agent tool returns flow through post-bash.sh or post-all.sh (or a new post-agent.sh) and remove the ambiguous '(or ...)'. Also specify the exact tool_name string(s) to match (e.g., 'Agent', 'computer', etc.) in the AC so the hook filter is unambiguous.

findings.location: Summary and Context sections
findings.finding: The plan references story '95f8-7ec4' and an implied technical consumer (agents/sub-agents in a consolidated dispatcher architecture), which satisfies the ANTI-FP carve-out for low-level/internal tooling. However, no named stakeholder or beneficiary is explicitly identified (e.g., 'DSO plugin authors', 'agent operators', 'orchestration pipeline maintainers'), and none of the acceptance criteria carry a concrete before/after validation mechanism or observable improvement to a named user — they are all system-internal checks (tests pass, hook registered, sentinel extracted). The story context is hidden behind an external script ('dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4'), so the value rationale cannot be independently verified here.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-sentence beneficiary statement to the Summary, e.g.: 'This enables agent orchestrators / DSO plugin authors to reliably capture sub-agent suggestions without manual log scraping.' Also add at least one AC that demonstrates observable value, e.g.: 'Given a sub-agent return containing a SUGGESTION: line, the orchestrating agent receives a recorded suggestion entry visible in [specific output/log/store] — confirming end-to-end signal propagation.'

findings.location: Summary — 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return', 'calls suggestion-record.sh'
Acceptance Criteria — all five bullets
findings.finding: Four of the five ACs are specific and in-session completable: the test-suite command is deterministic and runnable, sentinel extraction and warning behaviors are observable in code/output, dispatcher registration is a repo artifact, and edge-case test coverage is verifiable in-session. However, the second AC ('Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh') is slightly ambiguous on one point: 'Agent tool return' is not defined — it is unclear which tool name(s) or output format triggers the hook. Without knowing the shape of the PostToolUse payload the agent will see, an implementor cannot deterministically verify this criterion from the plan alone. This is thin but does not make the criterion effort-framed or post-sprint.
findings.suggested_edit: Specify the tool name(s) or payload field that identifies an 'Agent tool return' (e.g. 'tool_name == "Agent"' in the PostToolUse JSON), and state what constitutes a valid SUGGESTION: sentinel (regex or format), so the criterion is self-contained.

findings.location: Summary — 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)'; Acceptance Criteria — 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)'
findings.finding: The plan explicitly addresses its main failure mode (malformed sentinel) with a warn-not-crash requirement, which constitutes graceful degradation. The write operation (calling suggestion-record.sh) is a single append/record call; idempotency risk is low for a sentinel-recording script in this architecture. No external network calls or multi-step stateful transactions are described. The reliability posture is adequate for the scope of work described.

findings.location: Context section: 'Implement story 95f8-7ec4: PostToolUse hook on Agent tool returns that extracts SUGGESTION: sentinels.'
findings.finding: The plan references a story ID (95f8-7ec4) and defers full context to an external script ('dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4'), so the named stakeholder/beneficiary and the user problem are not directly visible in the plan text itself. The implied consumer appears to be developers/agents working within the DSO plugin architecture who need SUGGESTION sentinels surfaced from sub-agent returns — a plausible technical consumer — but it is never stated explicitly. Additionally, none of the acceptance criteria include a concrete validation mechanism that shows an observable improvement to that consumer (e.g., a before/after workflow comparison, an operational metric, or a dogfooding scenario). The test-suite pass criterion (bash tests/run-all.sh) is a correctness gate, not a value-validation mechanism.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a brief 'Who & Why' statement directly in the plan description, e.g.: 'As a DSO plugin developer/orchestrator, when a sub-agent returns a SUGGESTION: sentinel I currently have no automated way to capture it, causing suggestions to be silently lost. This hook ensures every suggestion is recorded, making them actionable.' Also add one AC like: 'Given a real sub-agent run that emits SUGGESTION: <text>, the suggestion is queryable via suggestion-record.sh output afterward (dogfood validation).'

findings.location: Summary: 'intercepts Agent tool returns, extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return, and records it via suggestion-record.sh'
Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding: Four of the five criteria are measurable and in-session completable: the test-suite command is deterministic, hook registration is inspectable in repo artifacts, and 'warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' is a verifiable behavioral outcome. However, two criteria carry minor ambiguity: (1) 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh' — 'first … per return' is the intended scope, but the plan never defines what constitutes a well-formed SUGGESTION: sentinel vs. a malformed one (the warn-on-malformed criterion depends on that boundary). Without a concrete format spec, an agent could implement any grammar and pass the tests. (2) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — 'edge cases' is not enumerated, making it subjectively completable; an agent might add a single test and call it done. Neither issue is blocking alone, but together they leave the agent with material discretion over what 'done' means.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-line sentinel format spec (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <non-empty freetext on the same line, no newline embedded') so malformed vs. well-formed is unambiguous. Replace 'edge cases' with an explicit list: empty SUGGESTION: value, multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first consumed), no SUGGESTION: line present, whitespace-only value.

findings.location: Summary — 'records it via suggestion-record.sh'; AC — 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)'
findings.finding: The plan adds two failure-prone operations: (a) a file/state write via suggestion-record.sh and (b) sentinel extraction with a warn-on-error path. For (a), the plan says nothing about what happens if suggestion-record.sh fails (non-zero exit, disk full, missing file, race condition). It is unclear whether the hook will propagate that failure to the caller, silently swallow it, or surface a warning. For (b), the plan specifies 'warn on malformed sentinel (no crash)' but does not say whether the hook returns a non-zero exit code in that case or always returns 0 — if the hook is registered in a PostToolUse dispatcher that halts on non-zero, an unexpected exit code could break unrelated tool calls. Idempotency of suggestion-record.sh on repeated invocations (e.g., if the hook fires twice for the same return) is also unaddressed.
findings.suggested_edit: Add explicit error-handling guidance: (1) If suggestion-record.sh exits non-zero, the hook should log a warning but itself exit 0 (so the dispatcher is not disrupted). (2) Specify that suggestion-record.sh is idempotent (e.g., deduplicates by content+timestamp key) so replay is safe. (3) State that malformed-sentinel warnings go to stderr and the hook exits 0.

findings.location: N/A
AC: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion for testing is too vague and fails several binary checks: (a) No failure/invalid paths are enumerated — 'edge cases' is not defined; it is unclear whether missing SUGGESTION lines, multiple SUGGESTION lines (only first should be taken), empty tool returns, or non-UTF-8/binary output are explicitly tested. (b) The malformed-sentinel warning path ('Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)') has no corresponding named test case or assertion — the AC says the warning must happen but does not say a test verifies it. (c) No observable/measurable outcome is declared for any test — what does success look like for the sentinel-extraction test? Exit code? File created by suggestion-record.sh? Specific stderr output for the malformed case? (d) RED mode requires a specific failing test to be named before implementation, but no test name or file location is cited for any of the required cases. (e) The integration with the consolidated dispatcher (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern) has no test coverage mentioned — dispatcher registration is an AC but has no corresponding test AC. Concretely missing: named test for (1) happy-path extraction, (2) multiple SUGGESTION lines → only first recorded, (3) malformed SUGGESTION → warning to stderr, no crash, suggestion-record.sh not called (or called safely), (4) no SUGGESTION line present → no call to suggestion-record.sh, (5) empty/null tool return, (6) dispatcher registration verification.
findings.suggested_edit:
Expand the testing AC to name specific test cases, e.g.:
- 'test_extract_first_suggestion: given output with two SUGGESTION: lines, only the first is passed to suggestion-record.sh'
- 'test_malformed_sentinel: given a line matching SUGGESTION: with missing required fields, a warning is printed to stderr and the script exits 0'
- 'test_no_suggestion: given output with no SUGGESTION: line, suggestion-record.sh is not called'
- 'test_empty_return: given empty tool output, no error and no call to suggestion-record.sh'
- 'test_dispatcher_registration: grep/structural assertion that the new hook is wired into post-bash.sh or post-all.sh'
Each test should declare its expected observable outcome (exit code, file creation, stderr content) and, per TDD RED mode, should reference the test file/function name that must fail before implementation.

findings.location: Plan description and Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance Criteria — 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The testing AC names only the happy-path bucket ('sentinel extraction edge cases') without enumerating specific failure/boundary scenarios, and none are pinned to a named RED test that fails before implementation. Concretely missing: (1) no SUGGESTION: line present at all (empty/no-sentinel return); (2) multiple SUGGESTION: lines — plan says 'first', but no test asserts only the first is recorded and the rest are ignored; (3) malformed sentinel (e.g. 'SUGGESTION:' with no trailing content, or with binary/non-UTF-8 payload) — the plan says 'warn on malformed, no crash' but no observable outcome (specific stderr message, exit code 0) is specified or asserted; (4) oversized sentinel value (extremely long line); (5) Agent tool return that is empty or null. Additionally, AC (a) 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' is a tautological gate — it tells the agent to keep running until the suite is green but does not declare what new tests must exist or what they must assert. Under TDD/RED mode, each new behaviour should have a named test file/case that is RED before the implementation is written.
findings.suggested_edit:
Replace the vague AC with concrete named tests, for example:
- `test_post_tool_use_no_sentinel`: Agent return with no SUGGESTION: line → suggestion-record.sh NOT called, exit 0.
- `test_post_tool_use_first_sentinel_only`: Agent return with two SUGGESTION: lines → suggestion-record.sh called exactly once with the first value.
- `test_post_tool_use_malformed_sentinel`: 'SUGGESTION:' with empty trailing content → stderr contains 'malformed sentinel', exit 0, suggestion-record.sh NOT called.
- `test_post_tool_use_happy_path`: Well-formed SUGGESTION: line → suggestion-record.sh called with exact value.
- Each test should mock/stub suggestion-record.sh to capture invocations (isolate external dep).
Also add a boundary test for an unusually long SUGGESTION value to guard against shell-variable truncation.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding:
The target file for the hook is not decided — the plan explicitly leaves it open with '(or post-all.sh)'. This is a real design choice deferred to the executor: post-bash.sh hooks only bash tool returns, while post-all.sh would intercept all tool types. Which file is correct depends on how 'Agent tool returns' surface in the dispatcher (as Bash tool output or as a distinct tool type), and that is not resolved in the plan. An executing agent cannot proceed without picking one, risking building in the wrong location entirely.

Additionally, 'first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return' leaves 'per return' undefined in terms of the dispatcher invocation model — it is unclear whether each invocation of post-bash.sh / post-all.sh represents exactly one tool return, or whether a single invocation may carry multiple returns that must be iterated.

The sentinel format itself is referenced but not specified (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <text>' — is there a schema, a max-length, characters allowed?). The 'malformed' condition that triggers a warning is also undefined — an agent cannot write a test for 'malformed' without knowing what 'well-formed' is.
findings.suggested_edit: Decide and state the target file (post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh) with a one-sentence rationale. Define the sentinel format (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <single-line text, no newlines, max 200 chars'). Define what constitutes 'malformed' (e.g., 'SUGGESTION:' with no trailing text, or text containing newlines). Clarify whether one dispatcher invocation equals one tool return.

findings.location: Summary: 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' and AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding:
(a) coupling_risk — The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (PostToolUse hook) that couples the dispatcher to suggestion-record.sh. This cross-component dependency is mentioned but not justified or mitigated. There is no interface/event boundary described; the hook will call suggestion-record.sh directly, making both components tightly coupled with no stated contract.

(b) changeability — 'First SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' is described as a fixed extraction rule. If the extraction policy (e.g., first vs. all, line format) needs to evolve, it appears it will be hardcoded in the shell script with no configuration point mentioned.

(c) documentation — The plan introduces a novel architectural decision: a new hook stage + sentinel protocol between agent tool returns and suggestion-record.sh. No ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc update is listed in the acceptance criteria. The CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher pattern is referenced but not documented here, and this extension of that pattern is not captured anywhere.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC: 'Update CLAUDE.md or an ADR to document the PostToolUse hook stage, the sentinel format contract, and the interface with suggestion-record.sh.' Consider extracting the sentinel pattern (format regex, extraction policy) into a shared config or sourced variable file so it can be updated without editing the hook itself.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The plan uses '(or post-all.sh)' to describe the target file, leaving the actual file selection unresolved. This is a genuine design choice deferred to the executor — it determines where the hook lives, how it is registered, and what other tools it affects. This triggers signal (3) conflicting signals and the PLACEHOLDER pattern ('or', 'TBD'). An executing agent would need to choose between two different integration points with meaningfully different scope (bash-only vs. all tools), which could lead to incorrect behaviour if the wrong file is chosen. Additionally, 'Agent tool returns' is not defined — it is ambiguous whether this means Claude's built-in Agent tool, a specific sub-agent invocation pattern, or something else. The sentinel format ('SUGGESTION: sentinel') is described only loosely; the malformed-sentinel warning criterion implies a specific expected format exists but it is never spelled out (no regex, no field count, no example). These gaps individually might be defaultable, but together they would likely cause an agent to stop and ask.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Replace 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' with a single, decided target file and justify the choice (e.g., 'post-all.sh because Agent tool is not bash-specific'). 2. Define 'Agent tool returns' precisely (e.g., 'tool_name == "Agent" in the PostToolUse hook payload'). 3. Provide the exact expected SUGGESTION: sentinel format (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <category>: <free-text>' with at least two colon-delimited fields) so the malformed-detection logic can be implemented unambiguously.

findings.location: Summary: 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' and AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: (a) Coupling risk: The plan introduces a new cross-component dependency — this hook calls suggestion-record.sh, a separate script, from inside the dispatcher pipeline. This dependency is mentioned but not justified or mitigated (no interface contract, no version pinning, no fallback if suggestion-record.sh is absent or fails). A silent hard dependency on an external script inside a dispatcher is a real coupling risk. (b) Changeability: The sentinel extraction logic (pattern matching, field count, warning threshold) appears to be hardcoded inside the hook script with no mention of configurability. If the sentinel format evolves, the hook must be edited directly. (c) Documentation: The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (PostToolUse → sentinel extraction → suggestion-record.sh) and a new pattern/contract (SUGGESTION: sentinel format). There is no mention of updating an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or any design doc to capture this architectural decision. This is a MAJOR gap under the documentation sub-check.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Add an AC to update CLAUDE.md or an ADR with the new pipeline stage, the SUGGESTION: sentinel contract (format, fields, version), and the dependency on suggestion-record.sh. 2. Make the sentinel regex/pattern configurable (e.g., via a DSO_SUGGESTION_PATTERN env var or a config file) rather than hardcoded in the hook. 3. Add an AC specifying graceful degradation if suggestion-record.sh is missing or returns non-zero (log a warning, do not crash the dispatcher).

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding: Only the first AC ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') has a concrete proving command. The remaining four ACs — (1) 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh', (2) 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)', (3) 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher', (4) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — carry no proving command at all. The generic `bash tests/run-all.sh` does NOT by itself prove each specific claim: it does not prove that the hook is registered in the dispatcher, that suggestion-record.sh is actually invoked, or that malformed-sentinel paths produce a warning rather than a crash. This is an anti-FP note in the criterion itself: 'universal lint/format/test commands do not by themselves prove a specific criterion.' Additionally, the plan body uses hedged language ('Add a PostToolUse hook … that intercepts … extracts …') with no concrete verification step (e.g., a targeted integration test invocation or manual end-to-end command) to demonstrate the sentinel-extraction path works. No end-to-end check of the 'Agent tool return → sentinel extracted → suggestion-record.sh called' flow is described anywhere in the plan.
findings.suggested_edit: For each remaining AC, add a concrete proving command or test reference. Examples: (AC2) 'Run `bash tests/hooks/test-post-tool-use-suggestion.sh`; assert suggestion-record.sh is invoked with the extracted value.' (AC3) 'Feed a malformed SUGGESTION: line to the hook and assert exit-code 0 plus a warning on stderr.' (AC4) 'Run `grep -n post-tool-use plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-all.sh` and assert a non-empty match.' (AC5) Enumerate the specific edge-case test names expected.

findings.location: Plan body ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)') vs AC ('Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher') vs AC ('Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases')
findings.finding:
Three coherence gaps found:

1. **Orphan work / ambiguous target file**: The plan body says 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — the parenthetical 'or' leaves the actual target file undecided. No AC names or verifies which file is modified, so the criterion 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' cannot be deterministically checked. The described deliverable (a specific file edit) has no unambiguous covering criterion.

2. **Terminology drift — 'PostToolUse' vs 'post-bash.sh / post-all.sh'**: The title and summary use 'PostToolUse hook', but the dispatcher files are named 'post-bash' and 'post-all'. The plan never reconciles whether a 'PostToolUse' event maps to the Bash dispatcher, the all-tools dispatcher, or both. The AC 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' uses yet a third phrasing ('consolidated dispatcher') without anchoring it to either filename.

3. **Uncovered deliverable — CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern**: The summary mentions 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' but no AC verifies this integration pattern is followed. This described design constraint is an orphan with no covering criterion.

No duplicate or near-duplicate criteria were found.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Resolve the 'or': commit to one target file (e.g., post-all.sh) in both the plan body and the AC verify step. 2. Add a terminology note mapping 'PostToolUse' → the chosen filename. 3. Add an AC or extend the existing dispatcher AC to verify the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern is present (e.g., 'Hook follows CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatch pattern, verified by code review of the relevant section in post-all.sh').

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding:
Only one of the five acceptance criteria has a concrete proving command: '[ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes' with its explicit verify shell invocation. The remaining four criteria have NO proving command at all:

1. 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh' — no command or test file named that would demonstrate this specific extraction behaviour.
2. 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' — no test case, no example invocation, no log-inspection command.
3. 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' — no grep/cat command to confirm registration; could be checked with e.g. `grep -r 'suggestion' plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/` but none is given.
4. 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — this claims test coverage exists but gives no list of edge cases and no command to confirm those specific tests exist beyond the generic run-all.

Additionally, criteria 1 and 4 are partially redundant with each other but criterion 4 is never independently verifiable from the plan text.

The generic `bash tests/run-all.sh` does NOT by itself prove criteria 2, 3, or the specific extraction behaviour in criterion 1 (anti-FP: universal test commands do not prove specific criteria). There is also no end-to-end check: a user-facing flow (the agent returning a SUGGESTION: sentinel triggering a recorded suggestion) is described but no integration smoke-test or rationale for its absence is provided.
findings.suggested_edit:
For each criterion, add a concrete proving command or reference a named test:
- Criterion 1: Name the specific test file and function, e.g. `bash tests/hooks/test-post-agent-suggestion.sh` and show the expected output.
- Criterion 2: Add `echo 'SUGGESTION:' | bash plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh 2>&1 | grep WARN` (or equivalent) as the verify step.
- Criterion 3: Add `grep -q 'extract_suggestion\|post_agent_suggestion' plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh && echo registered` as the verify step.
- Criterion 4: List the required edge cases (e.g., no sentinel present, multiple sentinels, sentinel with empty body, sentinel with extra whitespace) so the test suite can be objectively audited.
- Add an end-to-end smoke-test or document why one is impractical (e.g., requires a live sub-agent runtime).

findings.location: Plan body vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding:
Several coherence gaps exist:

1. **Orphan deliverable — target file ambiguity**: The plan body says 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — the '(or post-all.sh)' fork is never resolved. No acceptance criterion covers or names the actual target file, so the agent could legitimately implement in either file and satisfy every written criterion. This is a deliverable with no unambiguous covering criterion.

2. **Orphan deliverable — CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern**: The plan body mentions 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' but no acceptance criterion maps to this integration requirement. 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' is the closest criterion, but it does not reference or verify the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern at all — terminology drift between description and criteria.

3. **Criterion 4 ('Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases') has no edge-case list**: The described work says 'edge cases' but neither the plan body nor the criteria enumerate what those are. There is no described deliverable (e.g., a list of test stubs or scenarios) that maps to this criterion, making it unverifiable and essentially an orphan criterion.

4. **Terminology inconsistency**: The plan body calls the pattern 'PostToolUse hook' and also refers to 'post-bash.sh' and 'post-all.sh' interchangeably without resolution. The criterion says 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' — 'consolidated dispatcher' is introduced in the body but not defined, making cross-referencing ambiguous.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Resolve the 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' ambiguity in the plan body and name the single target file; add a criterion that explicitly names that file.
2. Add a criterion that maps to the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern integration, e.g., 'Hook follows CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher pattern as defined in [link/doc]'.
3. In criterion 4, replace 'edge cases' with an explicit list: at minimum — (a) no SUGGESTION: line present, (b) multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first recorded), (c) malformed sentinel body, (d) empty sentinel value.
4. Use consistent terminology: pick one name ('post-bash.sh' or 'post-all.sh', 'PostToolUse hook' or 'post-agent hook') and use it uniformly throughout.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five bullets
findings.finding: Only AC1 ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') provides a concrete proving command. The remaining four ACs lack specific proving commands: AC2 ('Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh'), AC3 ('Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)'), AC4 ('Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'), and AC5 ('Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases') are all stated as desired outcomes with no described command or artifact that would produce evidence of completion. The general test-suite command does not by itself prove AC2–AC4 individually (per anti-FP note). For example, AC3 requires a test that feeds a malformed sentinel and checks for a warning+exit-0 — no such check is identified. AC4 requires inspecting a dispatcher registration file/config — no command is cited. This leaves a gap between 'what we'll do' and 'what done looks like' for four of five criteria.
findings.suggested_edit: For each AC, add a concrete proving command: AC2: 'echo "SUGGESTION: foo" | bash plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-all.sh Agent && grep -q foo <suggestion-log>'; AC3: 'echo "SUGGESTION:" | bash post-all.sh Agent; [ $? -eq 0 ] && grep -i warn <stderr-capture>'; AC4: 'grep -q suggestion plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-all.sh (or equivalent registration config)'; AC5: list specific edge-case test file(s) that will be added.

findings.location: Summary and Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance Criteria — AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5
findings.finding: AC2 ('Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh') is effort-framed/implementation-framed rather than outcome-observable: it describes what the hook does internally, not what changes for the system in a verifiable way (e.g., 'suggestion-record.sh is invoked with the correct argument, confirmed by test output X'). AC3 ('Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)') is outcome-adjacent but does not specify what constitutes 'malformed', what the observable warning looks like (stderr message, exit code), or how it is measured. AC4 ('Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher') is a structural state claim with no observable artifact cited. AC5 ('Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases') is effort-framed ('cover edge cases') with no enumeration of which edge cases, making it impossible to evaluate completeness in-session. None of these four carry a deterministic in-session check.
findings.suggested_edit: Reframe each AC as an observable outcome: AC2: 'Running the hook against a tool return containing SUGGESTION: <text> causes suggestion-record.sh to be called with <text> as argument — verified by test assertion in tests/'; AC3: 'Running the hook against SUGGESTION: (no text) exits 0 and emits a warning to stderr — verified by a test'; AC4: 'post-all.sh (or post-bash.sh) contains a case/branch for Agent tool type that invokes the suggestion extractor — grep-verifiable'; AC5: enumerate edge cases: empty sentinel, multiple sentinels (only first taken), non-Agent tool (ignored), missing newline.

findings.location: Title, Summary, and Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding: Most acceptance criteria lack concrete proving commands beyond the single umbrella `bash tests/run-all.sh`. Specific criteria are unverified by that command alone: (1) 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh' — no command is given to demonstrate this end-to-end (e.g., invoking the hook with a crafted payload and asserting suggestion-record.sh is called). (2) 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' — no command or assertion is given to verify the warning is emitted and the process exits 0. (3) 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' — no grep/inspection command confirms registration. (4) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — a test suite pass does not prove coverage of edge cases unless the test names/assertions are enumerated. The umbrella test runner proves the suite passes, not that each specific criterion is reached.
findings.suggested_edit: For each criterion, add a concrete proving command or assertion. Examples: (1) `echo 'SUGGESTION: foo' | bash plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh Agent && grep 'foo' <suggestion-store>` for extraction; (2) `echo 'SUGGESTION:' | bash ... 2>&1 | grep -i warn && echo $?` for malformed-sentinel check; (3) `grep -r 'post-bash.sh\|post_tool_use' plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/` for registration; (4) enumerate edge-case test names explicitly in the plan.

findings.location: Summary and Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance Criteria — 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' and 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: Two criteria are effort/property-framed rather than outcome-observable: (1) 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' does not specify what observable artifact constitutes the warning (stderr message, log file entry, specific exit code) nor what 'malformed' means (empty value, missing colon, extra colons, unicode). Without this, it is untestable in-session in a deterministic way. (2) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' uses the subjective word 'cover' without enumerating which edge cases, making it impossible to verify completeness in-session. Both criteria also use hedged language ('cover' is vague) instead of specific observable outcomes.
findings.suggested_edit: Rewrite (1) as: 'When the hook receives a line matching `SUGGESTION:` with no following value, it prints a warning to stderr (matching pattern `WARN.*malformed`) and exits 0.' Rewrite (2) by listing the edge cases explicitly, e.g.: 'Tests assert correct behavior for: no SUGGESTION line, exactly one SUGGESTION line, multiple SUGGESTION lines (only first recorded), SUGGESTION with empty value (malformed warning), SUGGESTION with leading/trailing whitespace.'

findings.location: Title, Summary, and Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding: The title promises a PostToolUse hook that extracts SUGGESTION: sentinels from sub-agent returns and records them. The summary and criteria describe exactly that: intercepting Agent tool returns, extracting the first SUGGESTION: sentinel, recording via suggestion-record.sh, warning on malformed input, and integrating with the existing dispatcher architecture. There is no scope drift, no contradicting steps, and no omission of the headline goal. The only mild gap (no mention of existing callers that might be affected by adding a new hook to the dispatcher) is benign given the additive nature of hooks.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The target file is explicitly undecided — 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' defers a real architectural choice to the executor. Which dispatcher file receives the new hook directly affects the hook's trigger scope (Bash-only vs. all tools) and is not inferrable from the ticket alone without reading the full story context via a separate script. This is a 'choose an appropriate X' placeholder that blocks implementation planning. Additionally, the plan says 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return' but does not define what constitutes a 'malformed' sentinel beyond absence of one — the executor must guess the validation rules (e.g., missing colon, extra whitespace, multi-line value, empty value).
findings.suggested_edit: Decide and commit to one dispatcher file (e.g., 'Add the hook to post-all.sh because Agent tool returns are not Bash-specific'). Also enumerate malformed-sentinel conditions explicitly: e.g., 'SUGGESTION:' with no trailing text, 'SUGGESTION :' with a space before colon, or multiple SUGGESTION: lines (after the first).

findings.location: AC: 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' and Summary: 'records it via suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The plan introduces file I/O (recording via suggestion-record.sh) and a subprocess call, both of which are failure points. The plan states the hook should 'warn on malformed sentinel (no crash)' for one error case, but it does not address: (a) what happens if suggestion-record.sh itself fails (non-zero exit, missing file, permission error) — is the failure surfaced or swallowed? (b) whether recording is idempotent — if the hook fires twice for the same Agent return (e.g., due to dispatcher retry), will duplicate records be created? (c) graceful degradation if suggestion-record.sh is absent or not executable. Without idempotency and error-surface guarantees on the write path, the plan risks silent data corruption or duplicate state.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC: 'If suggestion-record.sh exits non-zero, the hook logs a warning and exits 0 (does not propagate failure).' Add a note on idempotency: 'suggestion-record.sh is safe to call multiple times with the same arguments' or describe deduplication logic in the hook.

findings.location: AC: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' and Summary description
findings.finding:
The testing AC is vague ('edge cases' is unspecified). The plan is in RED (TDD) mode but names no specific failing tests. Required checks:
(a) No specific RED test names or dependency edges are declared — the executor cannot know which test to write first.
(b) Failure/invalid paths not enumerated: missing SUGGESTION: line entirely, empty value, malformed format, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, non-UTF-8/binary output from sub-agent.
(c) The happy-path assertion ('calls suggestion-record.sh') is not measurable — how is the call verified? Stub? Spy? Exit-code check? Log check?
(d) No test for the 'no crash on malformed sentinel' AC is specified beyond the AC bullet itself.
(e) No test for the dispatcher registration is mentioned.
findings.suggested_edit: Add named RED tests, e.g.: 'test_extracts_first_suggestion_line — given output with one SUGGESTION: foo, assert suggestion-record.sh called with arg foo'; 'test_ignores_second_suggestion_line'; 'test_warns_on_empty_suggestion_value — SUGGESTION: with no trailing text logs warning to stderr, exits 0'; 'test_no_suggestion_line — hook exits 0, suggestion-record.sh not called'; 'test_dispatcher_registration — hook appears in dispatcher source'. Each test should declare its stub/spy mechanism for suggestion-record.sh.

findings.location: Summary: 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' and 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding:
The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (PostToolUse hook) into the consolidated dispatcher architecture and creates a new cross-component dependency (hook → suggestion-record.sh). Three sub-checks:
(a) Coupling risk: The dependency on suggestion-record.sh is introduced without justification of the interface contract (arguments, exit codes, expected side-effects). No interface boundary is described.
(b) Changeability: The sentinel format 'SUGGESTION:' is a business rule. No mention of whether this is configurable or hardcoded — if the sentinel prefix changes, every place it's matched must be updated.
(c) Documentation: The plan does not call for an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry for this new hook pattern in the dispatcher, despite it being a novel architectural decision (first PostToolUse hook on Agent returns in this dispatcher).
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC: 'Document the hook registration pattern and suggestion-record.sh interface in CLAUDE.md or an ADR.' Make the sentinel prefix a named constant or variable (e.g., SENTINEL_PREFIX='SUGGESTION:') rather than inlining it. Document the argument contract for suggestion-record.sh in the plan.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The target file for hook registration is explicitly unresolved — 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' defers the architectural decision to the executor. This is a scope placeholder, not a decision: the executor must 'figure out' which dispatcher to use before coding can proceed. Additionally, 'Agent tool returns' is undefined — it is unclear which tool name(s) trigger the hook (e.g., is 'Agent' a specific tool identifier in the consolidated dispatcher, or any sub-agent call?). The CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern mentioned gives no indication of which applies here, nor how it affects the hook's registration or output path.
findings.suggested_edit: Replace 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' with the definitive target file. Add a line naming the exact tool identifier(s) the hook should match (e.g., tool_name == 'Agent'). Clarify whether the hook participates in the CONFIDENT or UNCERTAIN pattern and what that means for its behavior.

findings.location: AC: 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' and 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The plan introduces file I/O (writing via suggestion-record.sh) and external script invocation (suggestion-record.sh). Two reliability gaps exist: (a) Error handling for suggestion-record.sh failure is not addressed — if the script exits non-zero (disk full, permission error, missing file), the plan only says 'warn on malformed sentinel' but is silent on what happens when the record write itself fails; there is no stated graceful degradation or surfacing of that error. (b) Idempotency of the write is unstated — if the hook fires twice for the same Agent tool return (e.g., retry scenario), suggestion-record.sh may record a duplicate. The plan does not declare whether duplicate writes are safe or how they are prevented.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC or implementation note: (a) 'If suggestion-record.sh exits non-zero, emit a warning to stderr and continue; do not propagate exit code.' (b) 'suggestion-record.sh writes are idempotent — duplicate invocations for the same return produce at most one recorded suggestion (e.g., keyed on a hash/ID).'

findings.location: AC: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' and Testing Mode: RED
findings.finding: The plan declares TDD (RED mode) but does not name any specific RED test. The only test-related AC is 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — a vague outcome, not a declared test. Required per criterion: each unit must name a specific RED test that fails before implementation. Concretely missing: (1) No named test for the happy path (single SUGGESTION: line present). (2) No named test for 'first sentinel only' when multiple SUGGESTION: lines exist. (3) No named test for malformed sentinel (e.g., 'SUGGESTION:' with no following text, or misspelled prefix). (4) No named test for no sentinel present (empty/absent). (5) No named test for suggestion-record.sh not being called when no sentinel is found. All five are observable, non-tautological scenarios directly implied by the ACs but none are concretely specified.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Test Cases' section with at minimum: test_extracts_first_suggestion_sentinel (multiple lines present → only first is passed to suggestion-record.sh), test_warns_on_malformed_sentinel (sentinel with empty value → stderr warning, no crash, suggestion-record.sh NOT called), test_no_sentinel_no_record (output with no SUGGESTION: line → suggestion-record.sh not invoked), test_suggestion_record_called_with_correct_args (assert exact args passed). Each test should be listed as a failing (RED) test name before implementation.

findings.location: Summary: 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' and 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (PostToolUse hook) that crosses component boundaries (hook dispatcher → suggestion-record.sh → wherever suggestions are stored/consumed). Three maintainability gaps: (a) Coupling risk: the dependency from the new hook on suggestion-record.sh's interface/contract is not documented or justified; if suggestion-record.sh's signature changes, silent breakage is likely. (b) Changeability: the sentinel format ('SUGGESTION:') is implicitly hardcoded in the hook logic with no mention of configurability or where it is canonically defined; if the sentinel string ever changes, it must be hunted across the codebase. (c) Documentation: no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc update is mentioned for this new hook pattern, despite it being a novel architectural decision (PostToolUse lifecycle hook writing to a suggestion store).
findings.suggested_edit: Add: (a) An AC requiring CLAUDE.md or an ADR entry to document the new hook pattern and the interface contract with suggestion-record.sh. (b) A note that the SUGGESTION: sentinel string is defined in a single canonical location (constant/config) referenced by both the hook and any consumers. (c) Explicit acknowledgment of the coupling to suggestion-record.sh with a stability note (e.g., 'suggestion-record.sh interface is stable/versioned').

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh) that intercepts Agent tool returns, extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return, and calls suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The plan introduces new I/O on what may be a hook hot-path (every Agent tool return triggers interception, sentinel extraction, and a call to suggestion-record.sh). No latency target or time-bound is stated for this path, and the I/O profile of suggestion-record.sh (e.g., whether it blocks on disk, network, or another process) is not described. Without codebase access it is unclear whether this hook fires synchronously inline with the tool-return path and whether suggestion-record.sh is a lightweight local write or a heavier operation.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a note clarifying (1) whether the hook runs synchronously or asynchronously relative to the tool-return path, (2) the expected worst-case latency of suggestion-record.sh, and (3) whether any concurrency guard is needed if multiple Agent tool returns arrive rapidly.

findings.location: Context: 'Implement story 95f8-7ec4'; Summary paragraph; Acceptance Criteria list
findings.finding: The plan defers the full user/problem context to an external command ('Read the full story context: .claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4') rather than stating it inline. No named stakeholder or end-user is identified in the plan itself, and none of the acceptance criteria include a concrete before/after workflow comparison or measurable outcome observable by a user. The implied consumer is a developer using the DSO plugin tooling, but this is never stated explicitly. Because this is an internal infrastructure/plugin task the bar is lower (implied technical consumer), but the absence of any validation mechanism (e.g., 'developer can now retrieve extracted suggestions from the suggestion store') still leaves value unverifiable from the plan text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Add one sentence naming the beneficiary (e.g., 'DSO plugin users / developers running sub-agent workflows') and one criterion with a concrete validation step such as 'given a sub-agent return containing a SUGGESTION: sentinel, the suggestion is observable in the suggestion store after the hook fires'.

findings.location: N/A
Summary: 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: Two issues of coherence/terminology are present: (1) The Summary hedges on the target file ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'), leaving the actual deliverable file ambiguous. The AC 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' does not resolve this ambiguity — neither phrase pins down which file is authoritative, so a reviewer cannot verify the criterion without guessing. (2) The AC 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' is a generic project-health gate rather than a criterion specific to this feature; it has no direct mapping to the described deliverable (sentinel extraction) and would pass even if the feature were absent. This makes it a near-orphan criterion relative to the described work. No duplicate criteria are present, and all other criteria map cleanly to described deliverables.
findings.suggested_edit: (1) Replace '(or post-all.sh)' with the definitive target file name before implementation begins. (2) Either remove the generic test-suite AC or make it explicit that it is a regression guard, and ensure at least one AC specifically asserts that the new hook's tests pass (which is partially covered by 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' but not tied to a pass/fail gate).

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh) that intercepts Agent tool returns, extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return, and calls suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The hook fires on every Agent tool return (potentially a high-frequency event depending on usage). The plan does not state whether suggestion-record.sh is synchronous I/O (file write, network call, etc.) or what its latency characteristics are. If it's a synchronous disk or IPC operation on every tool return, this could become a hot-path blocking concern. However, for a shell hook in a developer-tooling/CLI context, the volume is likely low enough that this is not a practical problem. Cannot confirm without inspecting suggestion-record.sh.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a brief note clarifying that suggestion-record.sh is a fast, local file-append operation (or similar), so the hook does not introduce blocking on the tool-return hot path.

findings.location: Context: 'Implement story 95f8-7ec4: PostToolUse hook on Agent tool returns that extracts SUGGESTION: sentinels.' / Summary / Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding: The plan references a story ID ('95f8-7ec4') and says the full context is available via an external command ('dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4'), but does not inline a named stakeholder or the user problem being solved. The implicit consumer is presumably a developer/agent working with sub-agent suggestions, but this is not stated. No before/after workflow comparison or operational metric target is present in the acceptance criteria — they are purely technical delivery checks (tests pass, hook registered, sentinel extracted). The value-validation mechanism (c,d checks) is absent from the inline plan text. This is a low-severity issue for an internal tooling task with an implied technical consumer, but the reliance on an external ticket for beneficiary context means the plan itself is incomplete as a standalone document.
findings.suggested_edit: Add 1–2 sentences to the Summary naming the stakeholder (e.g., 'Developer/operator using sub-agent workflows who needs suggestions surfaced automatically') and the problem (e.g., 'Without this hook, SUGGESTION: sentinels in sub-agent returns are silently dropped, requiring manual inspection'). Optionally add a criterion like: 'Given a sub-agent return containing SUGGESTION: foo, suggestion-record.sh is invoked and the suggestion is retrievable afterward.'

findings.location: N/A
Summary: 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; Acceptance Criteria: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: Two specific coherence issues: (1) The Summary names the target file ambiguously as 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — both are named in the description but the acceptance criterion 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' does not resolve which file is authoritative. This terminology drift means the implementing agent could make either choice and still claim the AC is met. (2) The AC 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' does not enumerate what edge cases are expected (e.g., missing sentinel, malformed but non-empty sentinel, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, empty return). The description mentions 'first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' and 'warn on malformed sentinel,' but the test AC does not reference these specific cases, leaving a gap between described behavior and coverage criterion. No duplicate criteria are present. No uncovered deliverables beyond these gaps.
